institutionName: North Carolina Agricultural and Technical State University
collegeName: College of Agriculture and Environmental Sciences (CAES)
programName: Bachelor of Science in Landscape Architecture
departmentChair: Dr. Niroj Aryal, Associate Professor and Chair
programDescription: The Bachelor of Science in Landscape Architecture at NC A&T is a rigorous, studio-intensive professional degree accredited by the Landscape Architectural Accreditation Board (LAAB). Across four years, students progress through a sequenced curriculum of 24 LDAR courses that build competencies in design fundamentals, ecological systems, site engineering, construction documentation, and collaborative professional practice. As the only LAAB-accredited program at a public HBCU, NC A&T uniquely positions graduates to bring underrepresented perspectives to environmental design, green infrastructure, and community-centered planning.

Purpose.label: Purpose & Self-Discovery
Purpose.description: Research, studio work, and foundational courses that build your design identity

Communities.label: Communities & Service
Communities.description: Collaborative projects, service-learning, and community engagement experiences

LocalGlobal.label: Local & Global Engagement
LocalGlobal.description: Study abroad, field work, and real-world site applications beyond campus

Identity.label: Professional Identity
Identity.description: Career prep, licensure readiness, portfolio development, and professional practice

careerOutlook.outlook: Landscape architects are in demand for green infrastructure, climate adaptation, and sustainable development. HBCU graduates bring unique perspectives to environmental justice and community-centered design.

sfricProjects.name: Inspiration Courtyard
sfricProjects.description: A contemplative outdoor space designed to honor agricultural heritage while showcasing sustainable materials, native plantings, and accessible design. Students contribute site analysis, planting design, construction documentation, and 3D visualization.

sfricProjects.name: Holland Bowl
sfricProjects.description: A significant landscape feature requiring grading analysis, stormwater management design, and ecological performance planning. Students engage with real terrain data, drone surveys, and hydrology modeling to develop implementable solutions.

sfricProjects.name: Woodland Restoration
sfricProjects.location: NC A&T University Farm — Woodland Edge
sfricProjects.description: A habitat corridor and woodland edge restoration project integrating ecological inventory, native plant communities, and biodiversity monitoring. Students conduct field research using SFRIC sensor data and drone imagery to design resilient woodland landscapes.

sfricProjects.name: Small Area Plan
sfricProjects.description: A community-scale planning effort developing land use, programming, and spatial frameworks for the SFRIC campus. Students conduct stakeholder interviews, precedent analysis, and phased master planning to create a comprehensive small area plan with implementation strategies.

aslaAwardCategories.name: General Design
aslaAwardCategories.description: Honors design excellence for built or conceptual projects at any scale — parks, plazas, campuses, streetscapes, and public spaces.

aslaAwardCategories.name: Residential Design
aslaAwardCategories.description: Celebrates exceptional design for private residential landscapes including gardens, estates, and multi-family outdoor spaces.

aslaAwardCategories.name: Analysis & Planning
aslaAwardCategories.description: Recognizes excellence in landscape planning, environmental analysis, and large-scale strategic projects.

aslaAwardCategories.name: Communications
aslaAwardCategories.description: Honors excellence in visual communication, graphic design, and public outreach in landscape architecture.

aslaAwardCategories.name: Research
aslaAwardCategories.description: Recognizes significant contributions to landscape architecture knowledge through original research.

aslaAwardCategories.name: Student Community Service
aslaAwardCategories.description: Celebrates student projects that demonstrate meaningful community engagement and service-learning in landscape architecture.

professionalOrgs.name: American Society of Landscape Architects (ASLA)
professionalOrgs.description: The national professional association — advocacy, awards, career resources, and student chapters

professionalOrgs.name: Black Landscape Architecture Network (BLAN)
professionalOrgs.description: Advancing Black professionals in landscape architecture — mentorship, community, and visibility
